role: A floating operator door that lets the user request a change to an indicated ui:// element by minting a build-intent, reviewing its X16 blast radius, and approving it for later dispatch.
capabilities: Render only when a ui:// element is indicated and its click mode is 'annotate'
Show the pointed element's title via the registry
Accept a free-text change description in a textarea
Mint a build-intent via AppContext.mintBuildIntent on button click or Ctrl/Cmd+Enter
Resolve the full persisted minted item from the live inbox by id (with flat-return fallback)
Display the X16 blast radius using the existing BlastRadiusReach component
Approve the minted intent via api.resolve
Dismiss/clear the minted state from the door
Show honest static safe-default / plan-mode copy
substance_why: The file implements a complete gated UI flow with local state, API calls, conditional rendering, keyboard handling, error display, and integration with the registry, AppContext, and BlastRadiusReach.
interactions_present: Indicate a ui:// element to open the door
Review blast radius (and navigate targets via BlastRadiusReach onNavigate)
interactions_missing: No live armed-state indicator (intentional, but also no way to arm from the FE)
No option to widen or edit the blast radius before approving
No edit/update of the intent text after minting
No cancel/abort while a request is in flight
No retry flow after a mint or approve failure
No handling if the indicated element changes while a minted intent is open
performance_risks: Linear scan of inbox.live_escalations on every render to resolve the minted item
No memoization of the resolved minted item or derived title
mintBuildIntent internally polls the inbox before returning, adding latency without visible progress beyond 'minting…'
BlastRadiusReach subtree may re-render whenever the door re-renders
reuse_why: The POINT → ASK → SEE THE REACH → APPROVE concept and safe-by-default semantics are worth preserving, but the component should be rebuilt around the new design system and its state/data resolution split out.
decomposition_hints: Extract a hook for resolving a minted intent from the inbox by id
Split the door into an 'Ask' form component and a 'Review/Approve' component
Move the safe-default / plan-mode messaging into a shared component or copy module
Keep BlastRadiusReach as a separate concern; do not inline reach rendering
Separate the approval state machine (idle, minting, reviewing, approved, error) from the presentation markup
